Implement the following plan:

# Downloads Folder Organization Plan

## Current State Analysis

- **Total files**: 260 files in Downloads root
- **Total folders**: 6 existing folders (auto-sort, temp, tools, etc.)
- **Total size**: ~2.3 GB
- **File types**: 55 PNG, 41 XLSX, 38 PDF, 29 MD, 17 PPTX, 10 JPG, 8 XMIND, plus others

## User Requirements

- **Cleanup level**: Conservative (keep everything in Downloads, organize into folders)
- **Organization method**: By project/client
- **Existing folders**: Leave unchanged (auto-sort, temp, tools, etc.)

## Proposed Folder Structure

```
Downloads/
├── 01-Projects-by-Client/
│   ├── Lululemon/
│   │   ├── Architecture-Diagrams/
│   │   ├── Presentations/
│   │   ├── Requirements/
│   │   ├── Technical-Design/
│   │   └── Other/
│   ├── Kering/
│   │   ├── Diagrams/
│   │   └── Requirements/
│   ├── BBY/
│   │   ├── Diagrams/
│   │   └── Presentations/
│   ├── Burberry/
│   │   └── Presentations/
│   └── WMP-Project/
│       ├── Requirements/
│       ├── Presentations/
│       └── Other/
├── 02-Internal-Systems/
│   ├── IODS/
│   │   ├── Architecture/
│   │   ├── Data-Modeling/
│   │   ├── API-Documentation/
│   │   └── Diagrams/
│   ├── Catalog-Service/
│   │   ├── Design-Docs/
│   │   ├── Presentations/
│   │   └── Requirements/
│   └── Baozun-Middleware/
│       ├── Capabilities/
│       └── Architecture/
├── 03-Documents-General/
│   ├── Presentations/
│   ├── Technical-Docs/
│   ├── HR-Admin/
│   └── Learning-Materials/
├── 04-Images-Screenshots/
│   ├── Screenshots/
│   ├── Diagrams-General/
│   └── Photos/
├── 05-Installers-Archives/
│   ├── Installers-DMG-PKG/
│   ├── Archives-ZIP/
│   └── Old-Installers/
├── 06-Code-Data-Files/
│   ├── JSON-CSV-Data/
│   ├── Config-Files/
│   ├── Code-Snippets/
│   └── Postman-Collections/
└── 07-To-Review/
    └── [files needing user decision]

[Existing folders remain untouched: auto-sort/, temp/, tools/, etc.]
```

## File Organization Rules

### Lululemon Files (largest project)
- **Keywords**: "lulu", "lululemon", "Lulu"
- **Architecture diagrams**: *.png, *.xmind, *.drawio → Lululemon/Architecture-Diagrams/
- **Presentations**: *.pptx, *.pdf with "SRE", "Search", "Catalog" → Lululemon/Presentations/
- **Requirements**: *.xlsx with "Requirements", "Feature list" → Lululemon/Requirements/
- **Technical design**: *.pdf, *.md with "design", "solution", "technical" → Lululemon/Technical-Design/
- **Migration docs**: Files with "migration" → Lululemon/Technical-Design/

### Kering Project
- **Keywords**: "kering"
- **Diagrams**: *.png, *.drawio → Kering/Diagrams/
- **Data files**: *.xlsx → Kering/Requirements/

### BBY Project
- **Keywords**: "bby", "BBY"
- **Diagrams**: *.png, *.xmind → BBY/Diagrams/
- **Presentations**: *.pptx → BBY/Presentations/

### Burberry Project
- **Keywords**: "burberry", "Burberry"
- **Presentations**: *.pptx → Burberry/Presentations/

### WMP Project
- **Keywords**: "WMP", "wmp"
- **Requirements**: *.xlsx, *.pptx → WMP-Project/Requirements/
- **Presentations**: *.pptx → WMP-Project/Presentations/

### IODS (Internal System)
- **Keywords**: "iods", "IODS", "china-iods"
- **Architecture**: *architecture*.png, *architecture*.pptx → IODS/Architecture/
- **Data modeling**: Files with "Data Model", "Schema", "Dwd" → IODS/Data-Modeling/
- **API docs**: *.md, *.json, files with "API", "Query", "Facade" → IODS/API-Documentation/
- **Diagrams**: *.png, *.drawio, *.xmind → IODS/Diagrams/

### Catalog Service
- **Keywords**: "catalog", "Catalog"
- **Design docs**: Files with "technical design", "design" → Catalog-Service/Design-Docs/
- **Presentations**: *.pptx → Catalog-Service/Presentations/
- **Requirements**: *.xlsx, files with "Requirements" → Catalog-Service/Requirements/

### Baozun General
- **Keywords**: "baozun", "middleware", "Baozun"
- **Architecture**: *.png with "architecture", "whole picture" → Baozun-Middleware/Architecture/
- **Capabilities**: Files with "capabilities" → Baozun-Middleware/Capabilities/

### General Documents (no specific project)
- **Presentations**: *.pptx, *.pdf (exclude project-specific) → Documents-General/Presentations/
- **Technical docs**: *.pdf with technical content → Documents-General/Technical-Docs/
- **HR/Admin**: Files with "insurance", "expense", "报销", "制度" → Documents-General/HR-Admin/
- **Learning**: Files with "practice", "guide", "tutorial" → Documents-General/Learning-Materials/

### Images & Screenshots
- **Screenshots**: Files with "Screenshot", "企业微信截图", "WeChat" → Images-Screenshots/Screenshots/
- **Diagrams**: *.png, *.jpg that are architectural diagrams → Images-Screenshots/Diagrams-General/
- **Photos**: IMG_*.jpg, IMG_*.png → Images-Screenshots/Photos/

### Installers & Archives
- **Installers**: *.dmg, *.pkg → Installers-Archives/Installers-DMG-PKG/
- **Archives**: *.zip → Installers-Archives/Archives-ZIP/
- **Old installers**: Installers from 2024 or earlier → Installers-Archives/Old-Installers/

### Code & Data Files
- **Data files**: *.csv, *.xlsx (non-requirements) → Code-Data-Files/JSON-CSV-Data/
- **Config files**: *.ini, *.xml, *.json (config) → Code-Data-Files/Config-Files/
- **Code snippets**: *.sql, *.json (non-config) → Code-Data-Files/Code-Snippets/
- **Postman**: *.postman_collection.json → Code-Data-Files/Postman-Collections/

### To Review (Uncategorized)
- Files with unclear purpose or generic names (111.csv, 1.txt, Untitled files) → 07-To-Review/

## Special Cases

### Duplicate Files
- ** enriched_sale_versionfinale2.xlsx** and **enriched_sale_versionfinale2 (1).xlsx**
  - Keep the newest (based on modification date)
  - Move the other to 07-To-Review/ with note

### Multiple Versions
- **catalog-technical-design*.pdf** (3 versions)
  - Keep all in Catalog-Service/Design-Docs/ (version control)

### Temporary/Config Files
- **.DS_Store**, **.localized**, **.$*** files → DELETE

### Very Generic Names
- **111.csv**, **1.txt**, **111.png**, **128.png**, **xx.txt**
  - Move to 07-To-Review/ for user inspection

## Execution Steps

1. **Create folder structure** (mkdir -p for all directories)
2. **Move project files** (iterate through file rules above)
3. **Handle duplicates** (identify and flag)
4. **Clean up temporary files** (remove .DS_Store, etc.)
5. **Generate summary report** (what moved where)

## Post-Organization Summary

After completion, you'll receive:
- **Files organized**: ~250 files into logical folders
- **Folders created**: ~40 organized subfolders
- **Files to review**: ~5-10 files needing your decision
- **Space recovered**: Minimal (conservative approach)
- **Deletions**: Only temporary/system files (.DS_Store, etc.)

## Verification

To verify the organization:
```bash
# Check folder structure
tree -L 3 /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads

# Count files in each major category
find /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads/01* -type f | wc -l
find /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads/02* -type f | wc -l
find /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads/03* -type f | wc -l
find /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads/04* -type f | wc -l
find /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads/05* -type f | wc -l
find /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads/06* -type f | wc -l

# List files needing review
ls -la /Users/tinker.chen/Downloads/07-To-Review/
```

## Safety Notes

- **No files deleted** (except system temp files like .DS_Store)
- **No files moved outside** of Downloads directory
- **Existing folders preserved** (auto-sort, temp, tools remain untouched)
- **All moves are reversible** (can move files back if needed)
- **User review required** for files in 07-To-Review/
